tweets.title:
We must look at this from another angle. When @VishnuNDTV says that Tejas Mk1A/Mk2 could be “obsolete on arrival,” a few important questions naturally arise:

What timeframe is he referring to?
Against which specific technologies or threats is this assessment being made?
Does he hold a similar view about Rafale F4 and other contemporary 4.5-generation fighters?
Is this assessment based on inputs from the IAF, simulations, operational analysis, or war-gaming outcomes?

We should keep the discussion respectful and focus on asking meaningful questions. That is how a healthy debate develops, where different perspectives are examined, assumptions are challenged, and everyone learns something new.
tweets.content:
We must look at this from another angle. When @VishnuNDTV says that Tejas Mk1A/Mk2 could be “obsolete on arrival,” a few important questions naturally arise:

What timeframe is he referring to?
Against which specific technologies or threats is this assessment being made?
Does he hold a similar view about Rafale F4 and other contemporary 4.5-generation fighters?
Is this assessment based on inputs from the IAF, simulations, operational analysis, or war-gaming outcomes?

We should keep the discussion respectful and focus on asking meaningful questions. That is how a healthy debate develops, where different perspectives are examined, assumptions are challenged, and everyone learns something new.

Raflanker 🇮🇳 (@raflanker)

I dont know what @VishnuNDTV sir knows…
But the comment was unwarranted or was it out of outright frustration due to delays?
And if he is saying Tejas obsolete, as a senior journalist he atleast give an informed view to support it, which is expected from such senior journalist.

— https://nitter.net/raflanker/status/2066395776453161278#m

A board constituted by Air Headquarter has initiated an investigation into the incident.

tweets.title:
G7 Summit Begins in France Today

The 52nd G7 Summit kicks off in Evian, France, with leaders set to discuss global security, economic challenges, technology, and ongoing geopolitical crises.

🇮🇳 India will participate as an invited "partner country" for the 13th time, with PM Narendra Modi joining the Outreach Sessions tomorrow.
tweets.content:
G7 Summit Begins in France Today

The 52nd G7 Summit kicks off in Evian, France, with leaders set to discuss global security, economic challenges, technology, and ongoing geopolitical crises.

🇮🇳 India will participate as an invited "partner country" for the 13th time, with PM Narendra Modi joining the Outreach Sessions tomorrow.

tweets.title:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
14th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief
 
●  Hezbollah launched 3 projectiles toward northern Israel, with impacts reported near the communities of Shomera and Shlomi.

● The IDF struck a Hezbollah command center in Dahieh, Beirut, following the launch of aerial targets toward Israel.

● US Vice President JD Vance and Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf are scheduled to hold a virtual meeting today to sign the MoU, after which the Strait of Hormuz is expected to reopen.

● All 14 Indian crew members were rescued safely after their vessel MSV Virat 1 began sinking off Oman, in a multinational operation involving Omani authorities and a US Navy aircraft.

● US Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth stated that Israel’s strike on Beirut is not expected to disrupt plans for signing the preliminary memorandum with Iran.

● Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei has notably withheld his formal approval of the draft MoU.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
12th Jun 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● The IDF carried out precision
strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure
in southern Lebanon, particularly around Bayt al-Siyad and Bint Jbeil.

● The Foreign Ministers of Iran and France discussed the latest regional developments, diplomatic efforts mediated by Pakistan, and bilateral issues.

● Leaders of Qatar, Pakistan, and the UAE called President Trump, urging him to refrain from striking Iran and stating that a deal with Tehran was within reach.

● According to Iranian sources, the proposed MoU with the US includes lifting sanctions, ending the naval blockade, and the withdrawal of US forces around Iran.

● US forces shot down two Iranian attack drones targeting commercial shipping, hours after President Trump called off planned strikes on Iran.

● As of today, CENTCOM has redirected 136 commercial vessels and disabled 9 to enforce compliance with the naval blockade on Iranian ports.

END OF THE BRIEF

OsintTV 📺 (@OsintTV)

🇮🇳 Breaking from Colonial Legacy, Indian Army Embraces Indian Identity.

📍The Indian Army has unveiled a new dress policy aimed at phasing out several colonial era traditions and symbols.

📍Senior officers will wear Indian-style bandi jackets at official functions, replacing the traditional lounge suit.

📍Reviewing Officers will no longer carry ceremonial swords during parades and inspections.

📍The ceremonial sword presentation tradition at commissioning ceremonies is being discontinued.

📍The "Royal" designation and other British-era references are being gradually removed from military traditions and institutions.

📍Officers have been instructed to avoid unnecessary display of medals, badges and ceremonial accessories during routine functions.

📍Uniformity in dress standards has been introduced across commands to promote a modern and professional appearance.

📍Visible tattoos and body piercings have been restricted in line with the new policy.

📍The Army has prescribed guidelines for hairstyles, grooming and appearance to maintain uniform standards.

📍Colonial customs associated with dress and ceremonial practices are being phased out.

📍The reforms are part of the Army's broader effort to decolonise military traditions and strengthen a distinctly Indian identity.

From bandi jackets to the end of ceremonial swords and royal tags, the Indian Army is turning the page on its colonial past and embracing its own traditions.

— https://nitter.net/OsintTV/status/2066150841057210792#m

tweets.title:
RT by @alpha_defense: Everytime a discussion on India’s journey in frontier tech comes, the opinion is always divided on which way India should go. The American way(state largesse funded SOTA basic research in academia and industry picking up the baton and then running the next 1000 miles with its cash flows) or China way(state funded, state directed largesse and state controlled but pvt sector executed). 

India has structural problems in pursuing either of the way that we are not ready to discuss head on but beat around with either “look at SpaceX Google, Indian pvt sector should pick up” or “look at China, all are govt funded efforts under writing the risk”. In the end we come up with a lame ass namesake answer of frugal engineering that never really solves for the mission at hand but just make does with the status quo and tries to produce whatever we can. 

Because the constraint here is about solving for talent, resources and capital with the latter taking care of the former two of enough of it is thrown at the problems. Who should solve for the capital problem in India’s case today if India’s stated ambition today is sovereignty and strategic autonomy? 

India needs a new compact and that needs to look at India from first principles, Indian state, public and the industry shedding certain aspects and pursuing newer mechanisms. 

State banks giving zero interest loans? Govt establishing sovereign fund with full risk appetite? Govt directing CSR funds for fundamental research? Tax holidays? India’s equivalent of thousand talents program? India’s equivalent of annually multi billion $$ NSF? Special Research Zones along lines of GIFT city? 

More deeper stuff like allowing freer capital flows, getting rid of reservation and permanent cadre in key STEM research orgs and bringing hire and fire with lateral entries, pay structure changes, a stated goal to  achieve a 3X GOI spending on R&D from current levels in a PPP mode so that GOI ₹₹ acts as catalyst for pvt sector to put in their share and above this bringing restrictions on foreign tech use at strategic orgs. 

Ppl who try to dampen this with demanding expectations commensurate with India’s PCI, cost of sovereignty doesn’t track PCI. It costs whatever it takes to have strategic autonomy. Those expecting sovereign capabilities at frugal costs and without structural reforms are essentially demanding free lunch for an unserious player.
tweets.content:
Everytime a discussion on India’s journey in frontier tech comes, the opinion is always divided on which way India should go. The American way(state largesse funded SOTA basic research in academia and industry picking up the baton and then running the next 1000 miles with its cash flows) or China way(state funded, state directed largesse and state controlled but pvt sector executed).

India has structural problems in pursuing either of the way that we are not ready to discuss head on but beat around with either “look at SpaceX Google, Indian pvt sector should pick up” or “look at China, all are govt funded efforts under writing the risk”. In the end we come up with a lame ass namesake answer of frugal engineering that never really solves for the mission at hand but just make does with the status quo and tries to produce whatever we can.

Because the constraint here is about solving for talent, resources and capital with the latter taking care of the former two of enough of it is thrown at the problems. Who should solve for the capital problem in India’s case today if India’s stated ambition today is sovereignty and strategic autonomy?

India needs a new compact and that needs to look at India from first principles, Indian state, public and the industry shedding certain aspects and pursuing newer mechanisms.

State banks giving zero interest loans? Govt establishing sovereign fund with full risk appetite? Govt directing CSR funds for fundamental research? Tax holidays? India’s equivalent of thousand talents program? India’s equivalent of annually multi billion $$ NSF? Special Research Zones along lines of GIFT city?

More deeper stuff like allowing freer capital flows, getting rid of reservation and permanent cadre in key STEM research orgs and bringing hire and fire with lateral entries, pay structure changes, a stated goal to achieve a 3X GOI spending on R&D from current levels in a PPP mode so that GOI ₹₹ acts as catalyst for pvt sector to put in their share and above this bringing restrictions on foreign tech use at strategic orgs.

Ppl who try to dampen this with demanding expectations commensurate with India’s PCI, cost of sovereignty doesn’t track PCI. It costs whatever it takes to have strategic autonomy. Those expecting sovereign capabilities at frugal costs and without structural reforms are essentially demanding free lunch for an unserious player.

tweets.title:
Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami chief Shafiqur Rahman has called on the government to seek clarification from India's High Commissioner-designate Dinesh Trivedi over remarks suggesting India and Bangladesh should "become one" in their efforts.

Rahman stressed that both nations are sovereign and independent states, warning that any ambiguity over the statement could create confusion among the public.
tweets.content:
Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami chief Shafiqur Rahman has called on the government to seek clarification from India's High Commissioner-designate Dinesh Trivedi over remarks suggesting India and Bangladesh should "become one" in their efforts.

Rahman stressed that both nations are sovereign and independent states, warning that any ambiguity over the statement could create confusion among the public.

tweets.title:
🚨 From Battlefield Command to Military Transformation: Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth Set to Lead the Indian Army

📍Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth will take over as the next Chief of Army Staff on June 30.

📍He has commanded two operational Army Commands, South Western Command and Southern Command, a rare distinction among Indian Army officers.

📍Played a key role in driving force modernisation and integrating emerging technologies into the Army's future warfighting doctrine.

📍Brings nearly 40 years of military experience across conventional and counter insurgency operations.

📍Has commanded the prestigious Sudarshan Chakra Corps, an Armoured Brigade and a Counter Insurgency Force in J&K.

📍Currently serves as the Vice Chief of Army Staff.

📍An NDA alumnus, he was commissioned into the Armoured Corps in 1986.

📍Has undergone advanced military training in India and France, including the National Defence College.

India's next Army Chief takes charge at a time when the Indian military is rapidly transforming to meet future challenges
tweets.content:
🚨 From Battlefield Command to Military Transformation: Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth Set to Lead the Indian Army

📍Lt Gen Dhiraj Seth will take over as the next Chief of Army Staff on June 30.

📍He has commanded two operational Army Commands, South Western Command and Southern Command, a rare distinction among Indian Army officers.

📍Played a key role in driving force modernisation and integrating emerging technologies into the Army's future warfighting doctrine.

📍Brings nearly 40 years of military experience across conventional and counter insurgency operations.

📍Has commanded the prestigious Sudarshan Chakra Corps, an Armoured Brigade and a Counter Insurgency Force in J&K.

📍Currently serves as the Vice Chief of Army Staff.

📍An NDA alumnus, he was commissioned into the Armoured Corps in 1986.

📍Has undergone advanced military training in India and France, including the National Defence College.

India's next Army Chief takes charge at a time when the Indian military is rapidly transforming to meet future challenges

tweets.title:
RT by @OsintTV: Another inhumane act by the Pakistani Army and Rangers, they vandalized and looted a bakery belonging to an Awami Action Committee activist in Rawlakot , Pakistan-occupied Jammu Kashmir.

The bakery was closed, but because it displayed the Awami Action Committee logo so troops damaged property inside and outside the shop and looted bakery items before leaving.

As protests enter their fifth consecutive day, people from across PoJK continue to arrive at Eidgah Ground in Rawalakot. Women and children remain at the forefront of the movement despite the ongoing massacre.

#PoJK #Rawalakot #PakistanArmy #AwamiActionCommittee
